volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Philoctetes (Section 782 - Section 906)

sections.label: Section 782
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὖν δέδοικα μὴ ἀτέλεστʼ εὔχῃ , τέκνον .
sections.translation: Futile , I fear , are your prayers , boy . Look , once again the dark blood is oozing drop by drop from deep in the wound , and I look for worse to come .

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: παπαῖ , φεῦ .
sections.translation: Ah , me , oh , oh ! Cursed foot , what torment you cause me ! It creeps on me , it is coming near ! Ah , misery ! Now you know my condition . Do not flee , no !

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: ἀτταταῖ .
sections.translation: Oh , alas ! Odysseus of Cephallene , once my friend , would that this anguish might stick to you , and pierce your chest ! Ah , me ! Ah , me ! O you twin marshalls , Agamemnon , and you , Menelaus , may your flesh instead of mine

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: τὸν ἴσον χρόνον τρέφοιτε τήνδε τὴν νόσον ; ἰώ μοι .
sections.translation: nourish this plague , and for as long ! Oh , Ah , me ! O Death , Death , though I am always summoning you day after day , why do you never come ? O son , noble youth , seize me ,

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: τῷ Λημνίῳ τῷδʼ ἀνακαλουμένῳ πυρὶ
sections.translation: burn me up , true friend , in that fire famed as Lemnian . I , too , once deemed it lawful to do that very service for the son of Zeus , in return for which I received these same arms , which are now in your keeping . What do you say , boy , what do you say ?

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: τί φής ; τί σιγᾷς ; ποῦ ποτʼ ὤν , τέκνον , κυρεῖς ;
sections.translation: Why this silence ? Where are your thoughts , son ?

sections.label: Section 806
sections.original: ἀλγῶ πάλαι δὴ τἀπὶ σοὶ στένων κακά .
sections.translation: My heart has long been aching for your load of pain .

sections.label: Section 807
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ τέκνον , καὶ θάρσος ἴσχʼ · ὡς ἥδε μοι
sections.translation: Stop , then , and take courage ; this visitor comes sharply , but goes quickly . Yet , I beg you , do not leave me alone .

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: θάρσει , μενοῦμεν .
sections.translation: Take heart , we will remain .

sections.label: Section 810a
sections.original: ἦ μενεῖς ;
sections.translation: Will you ?

sections.label: Section 810b
sections.original: σαφῶς φρόνει .
sections.translation: Be sure of it .

sections.label: Section 811
sections.original: οὐ μήν σʼ ἔνορκόν γʼ ἀξιῶ θέσθαι , τέκνον .
sections.translation: Indeed , I do not think it right to put you under oath .

sections.label: Section 812
sections.original: ὡς οὐ θέμις γʼ ἐμοὔστι σοῦ μολεῖν ἄτερ .
sections.translation: Rest assured ; it is not lawful for me to leave without you .

sections.label: Section 813
sections.original: ἔμβαλλε χειρὸς πίστιν .
sections.translation: Give me your hand in pledge !

sections.label: Section 813a
sections.original: ἐμβάλλω μενεῖν .
sections.translation: I give it — to stay .

sections.label: Section 814
sections.original: ἐκεῖσε νῦν μʼ , ἐκεῖσε
sections.translation: Now take me there , over there !

sections.label: Section 814a
sections.original: ποῖ λέγεις ;
sections.translation: Where do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 814b
sections.original: ἄνω
sections.translation: Up there !

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: τί παραφρονεῖς αὖ ; τί τὸν ἄνω λεύσσεις κύκλον ;
sections.translation: What is this new delirium ? Why do you gaze at the dome above us ?

sections.label: Section 816
sections.original: μέθες μέθες με .
sections.translation: Let me go , let me go !

sections.label: Section 816a
sections.original: ποῖ μεθῶ ;
sections.translation: Where will you go , if I do so ?

sections.label: Section 816b
sections.original: μέθες ποτέ .
sections.translation: Let me go , I say !

sections.label: Section 817
sections.original: οὔ φημʼ ἐάσειν .
sections.translation: I will not .

sections.label: Section 817a
sections.original: ἀπό μʼ ὀλεῖς , ἢν προσθίγῃς .
sections.translation: You will kill me , if you touch me further .

sections.label: Section 818
sections.original: καὶ δὴ μεθίημʼ , εἴ τι δὴ πλέον φρονεῖς .
sections.translation: There , then , I release you — if in fact you believe it is for the better .

sections.label: Section 819
sections.original: ὦ γαῖα , δέξαι θανάσιμόν μʼ ὅπως ἔχω
sections.translation: Wide Earth , embrace me now on the verge of death !

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: τὸ γὰρ κακὸν τόδʼ οὐκέτʼ ὀρθοῦσθαί μʼ ἐᾷ .
sections.translation: This pain no longer lets me stand up .

sections.label: Section 821
sections.original: τὸν ἄνδρʼ ἔοικεν ὕπνος οὐ μακροῦ χρόνου
sections.translation: Sleep , I think , will take him before long . See , his head sinks backward . Yes , a sweat runs over his whole body , and a dark , hemorrhaging vein has burst from his heel .

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: αἱμορραγὴς φλέψ . ἀλλʼ ἐάσωμεν , φίλοι ,
sections.translation: Come , friends , let us leave him in quietness , so that he may fall asleep .

sections.label: Section 1.827
sections.original: Ὕπνʼ ὀδύνας ἀδαής , Ὕπνε δʼ ἀλγέων ,
sections.translation: Divine Sleep , god who knows no pain , Sleep , stranger to anguish , come in favor to us , come happy , and giving happiness , great King !

sections.label: Section 1.830
sections.original: ὄμμασι δʼ ἀντίσχοις
sections.translation: Keep before his eyes such light as is spread before them now . Come to him , I pray you , come with power to heal ! Son , consider what position you will take , and to what strategy you will next direct our course .

sections.label: Section 1.835
sections.original: φροντίδος . ὁρᾷς ἤδη .
sections.translation: You see his condition now ! Why should we hesitate to act ? Opportunity , the umpire of all contests , often wins a great victory by one swift stroke .

sections.label: Section 839
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὅδε μὲν κλύει οὐδέν , ἐγὼ δʼ ὁρῶ οὕνεκα θήραν
sections.translation: No , even though he hears nothing , I see that

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: τήνδʼ ἁλίως ἔχομεν τόξων , δίχα τοῦδε πλέοντες .
sections.translation: we have made this bow our quarry to no end , if we sail without him . His must be the victor ’ s crown . It is he that the god commanded we bring . It would be a foul disgrace upon us to boast of deeds in which failure and fraud had equal parts .

sections.label: Section 1.843
sections.original: ἀλλά , τέκνον , τάδε μὲν θεὸς ὄψεται ·
sections.translation: Well , the god must look to that , son . But when you answer me again ,

sections.label: Section 1.845
sections.original: βαιάν μοι , βαιάν , ὦ τέκνον ,
sections.translation: softly , please , softly whisper your words . For sick men ’ s restless sleep is quick to vision . But , please , use your utmost care to win that prize ,

sections.label: Section 1.850
sections.original: κεῖνο δή μοι κεῖνο λάθρᾳ
sections.translation: that great prize , by stealth . For if you maintain your present purpose towards this man — you know what purpose I mean — then truly there are irremediable troubles in store , which a shrewd mind can foresee .

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: οὖρός τοι , τέκνον , οὖρος ·
sections.translation: Now , son , a fair wind blows you on your way : sightless and helpless , the man lies stretched in darkness — sleep in the heat is sound —

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: οὐ χερός , οὐ ποδός , οὔ τινος ἄρχων ,
sections.translation: with no command of hands or feet , but stripped of all his powers , like one who rests with Hades . Take note , see if your pronouncements are seasonable . So far as my thoughts can seize the truth , boy , the best strategy is that which stirs no alarm .

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: σιγᾶν κελεύω μηδʼ ἀφεστάναι φρενῶν ·
sections.translation: Quiet , I say , and do not abandon your wits — the man is opening his eyes and lifts his head .

sections.label: Section 867
sections.original: ὦ φέγγος ὕπνου διάδοχον τό τʼ ἐλπίδων
sections.translation: Ah , light succeeding upon sleep ! Ah , friendly watchers , sight unlooked for even in my dreams ! I could never have expected this , son —

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: τλῆναί σʼ ἐλεινῶς ὧδε τἀμὰ πήματα
sections.translation: that you would have the patience to wait so tenderly upon my sufferings by staying close beside me and helping to relieve me . The Atreids , certainly , those valiant generals , had no heart to bear this burden so lightly . But your nature is noble and drawn of a noble line ,

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , ἡ σή , πάντα ταῦτʼ ἐν εὐχερεῖ
sections.translation: and so you have made little of all this , though loud cries and stenches afflicted you . And now , since the plague seems to allow me a space of forgetfulness and peace at last , lift me up yourself and set me on my feet ,

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: ἵνʼ , ἡνίκʼ ἂν κόπος μʼ ἀπαλλάξῃ ποτέ ,
sections.translation: so that , when the faintness releases me at length , we may set out to the ship , and not hold back our sailing .

sections.label: Section 882
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἥδομαι μέν σʼ εἰσιδὼν παρʼ ἐλπίδα
sections.translation: It pleases me to see you living unpained and breathing still , however contrary to my expectation . For in view of your persistent sufferings ,

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: πρὸς τὰς παρούσας ξυμφορὰς ἐφαίνετο .
sections.translation: your symptoms seemed to speak of death . But now lift yourself , or , if you prefer , these men will carry you . The labor will not be grudged , since you and I are of one mind .

sections.label: Section 889
sections.original: αἰνῶ τάδʼ , ὦ παῖ , καί μʼ ἔπαιρʼ , ὥσπερ νοεῖς ·
sections.translation: Thank you , son , and help me up , as you will .

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: τούτους δʼ ἔασον , μὴ βαρυνθῶσιν κακῇ
sections.translation: Yet do not trouble your men , so that they may not suffer from the foul stench before it is necessary . It will be trial enough for them to live on board with me .

sections.label: Section 893
sections.original: ἔσται τάδʼ · ἀλλʼ ἵστω τε καὐτὸς ἀντέχου .
sections.translation: So be it . Now stand up , and take hold of me .

sections.label: Section 894
sections.original: θάρσει · τό τοι σύνηθες ὀρθώσει μʼ ἔθος .
sections.translation: Do not worry , my habitual method will help me to my feet .

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: παπαῖ · τί δῆτʼ ἂν δρῷμʼ ἐγὼ τοὐνθένδε γε ;
sections.translation: God ! What am I to do next ?

sections.label: Section 896
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν , ὦ παῖ ; ποῖ ποτʼ ἐξέβης λόγῳ ;
sections.translation: What is the matter ? Where strays your speech ?

sections.label: Section 897
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ ὅποι χρὴ τἄπορον τρέπειν ἔπος .
sections.translation: I do not know where to turn my tongue when my thoughts are so confused .

sections.label: Section 898
sections.original: ἀπορεῖς δὲ τοῦ σύ ; μὴ λέγʼ , ὦ τέκνον , τάδε .
sections.translation: Confused ? How so ? No , do not say it !

sections.label: Section 899
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐνθάδʼ ἤδη τοῦδε τοῦ πάθους κυρῶ .
sections.translation: My mind is indeed brought to that condition .

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: οὐ δή σε δυσχέρεια τοῦ νοσήματος
sections.translation: It cannot be that offense at my sickness has persuaded you not to take me aboard your ship ?

sections.label: Section 902
sections.original: ἅπαντα δυσχέρεια , τὴν αὑτοῦ φύσιν
sections.translation: All is offense when a man has abandoned his true nature and does what does not suit him .

sections.label: Section 904
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδὲν ἔξω τοῦ φυτεύσαντος σύ γε
sections.translation: But you , at least , are not departing from your begetter ’ s

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: δρᾷς οὐδὲ φωνεῖς , ἐσθλὸν ἄνδρʼ ἐπωφελῶν .
sections.translation: example either in word or deed , when you help a man who is noble .

sections.label: Section 906
sections.original: αἰσχρὸς φανοῦμαι · τοῦτʼ ἀνιῶμαι πάλαι .
sections.translation: I shall be found to have no honor — this is the thought that long torments me .
